story.iWant: to be alerted when an internal-only label (criterion name, parameter slug, scoring-axis identifier) leaks into a learner-visible surface at runtime
story.soThat: the live IELTS Part 3 / Mock leak class (#1955 / PR #2134) is caught even when it flows through templating, props, or other dynamic paths that the static-literal Coverage gate (PR #2144) can't see

context.applies: Every pipeline run after COMPOSE has produced a ComposedPrompt. The runner scans the composed prompt body + any PinnedCardContent stamped on the session.
dependsOn: PR #2153 — #2145 Phase A SessionFocus substrate (the projection path this gate watches)
assumptions: The static-literal Coverage gate (apps/admin/tests/lib/sim-chat/learner-ui-leak-coverage.test.ts) catches HARDCODED leaks at build time.

This runtime gate catches the COMPLEMENTARY class — labels that reach the learner surface via dynamic projection (props, templated strings, copy-paste from internal data into PinnedCardContent.focusArea or tutor prompt).
Both gates read the SAME registry JSON — one source of truth. Adding a label there extends both gates simultaneously.

parameters.name: Internal-Label Leak Count
parameters.description: Number of unique internal-only labels detected in learner-visible surfaces during this pipeline run. SUPERVISE-alarm signal — operator-only; no AGGREGATE / ADAPT / REWARD consumer (the alarm is read by humans via AppLog, not folded into the cascade).

config.scoreSemantics: Unique-leak count. score = uniqueLeakCount (e.g. 0 → no write; 2 → score=2). NOT normalised to 0-1; the operator reads the raw count from AppLog.metadata when triaging the alarm.

acceptanceCriteria.title: Leak detected → CallScore + AppLog
acceptanceCriteria.given: A composed prompt containing one or more internal-only labels from the shared registry
acceptanceCriteria.then: writeCallScore is invoked with parameterId=BEH-INTERNAL-LEAK and score equal to the unique-leak count; AppLog subject 'supervise.internal_leak_detected' is emitted with per-leak detail (label, setKey, surface, sessionId, callerId)

acceptanceCriteria.title: No leaks → silent no-op
acceptanceCriteria.given: A composed prompt with NO internal-only labels (the happy path)
acceptanceCriteria.then: No CallScore row is written; no AppLog row is emitted (per the honest-empty-state contract — fake zero-scores corrupt downstream EMA)

acceptanceCriteria.title: Empty registry → silent no-op
acceptanceCriteria.given: The shared registry JSON contains no label sets (or all sets are empty)
acceptanceCriteria.then: No CallScore row is written; no AppLog row is emitted; the runner returns cleanly without throwing

constraints.description: The runner MUST NOT write a zero CallScore on the happy path. Per ~/.claude/projects/-Users-paulwander-projects-HF/memory/feedback_no_hardcoded_score_backfill.md — honest empty rows are operator-actionable; fake-zero scores corrupt EMA and mask architectural gaps.

The runtime gate (this spec) and the build-time gate (apps/admin/tests/lib/sim-chat/learner-ui-leak-coverage.test.ts) MUST read the SAME JSON. A divergence test pins this — adding a label in one place auto-extends both gates.
